description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: test, staging, and production, and define variables APP_NAME, APP_NAME_STAGING, APP_PORT, APP_TEST_LOCAL, and APP_TEST_STAGING. In the test stage, add a test job using the python:3-alpine image that runs before_script commands `apk add py-pip py-setuptools`, `pip install requests flask`, `export FLASK_APP=${APP_NAME}.py`, `export FLASK_RUN_PORT=${APP_PORT}`, `python -m flask run &`, `echo 'Give it some time to start' && sleep 2`, and then executes `python test.py ${APP_TEST_LOCAL}`; it runs only on changes to .gitlab-ci.yml or *.py and is excluded from the master branch. In the staging stage, add a staging job using the tmaier/dpl image with a before_script that updates apk, installs python, py-pip, py-setuptools, git, openssh-client, installs the Python requests package, configures git user name/email, sets the remote URL, sets up SSH agent with the $SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, and disables strict host key checking; the script runs `dpl --provider=heroku --app=$APP_NAME_STAGING --api-key=$HEROKU_API_KEY`, `echo 'Give it some time to start' && sleep 5`, `python test.py ${APP_TEST_STAGING}`, `git commit --allow-empty -m "Staging succeeded for ${CI_COMMIT_SHA}"`, and `git push -f origin HEAD:master`; it runs on any branch except master when .gitlab-ci.yml or *.py files change. In the production stage, add a production job also using the tmaier/dpl image that runs only on the master branch and executes `dpl --provider=heroku --app=$APP_NAME --api-key=$HEROKU_API_KEY` to deploy the main app to Heroku. No caches, artifacts, or services are defined.
